title: Probe qPCR assay for Charybdis japonicum
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c0dr0","text":"Charybdis japonica or the Asian Paddle Crab is a non-native crab species to Aotearoa, New Zealand. It was first detected in 2000 and has since spread along the eastern Northland coast where it is a threat to local native species. This test was developed to aid in detecting the invasive crustacean's presence using environmental DNA sampling. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a8e7h","text":"The probe based qPCR assay targets a 208bp portion of the COX-1 gene with an indicative limit of detection of 1.0E-4 ng/µl genomic DNA input or 92 molecules/µl with a synthetic standard (IDT gBlock). Species specificity was successfully challenged against a panel of 14 New Zealand native crab species. Further challenge with local species is recommended. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 10.0 µl SensiFAST Probe 2x Mix 0.8 µl 10µM Forward primer 0.8 µl 10µM Reverse primer 0.2 µl 10µM FAM Probe 6.2 µl PCR Water 2.0 µl Template 20µl total reaction volume
95°C - 3 minutes 95°C - 15 seconds 60°C - 30 seconds x45 cycles QuantStudio 3 (ThermoFisher Scientific) used during assay development.

Meridian Bioscience SensiFAST Probe Lo-ROX Mix (2x) PCR quality water Template DNA Oligonucleotides (10uM concentration): Forward primer: Cj_1295FATGGCATGCCTCGTCGTTAT Reverse primer: Cj_1498RCTGCAGGTGGGTAAGAGTGG FAM Probe: Cj_1323PCCCCGATGCATACACTACGT

Many thanks to the National Institute of Water and Atmospheric Research (NIWA), the Marine Invasives Taxonomic Service (MITS) and Deakin University for contributions from their invertebrate tissue collection.
